selection_policy: Select exactly one profile by semantic intent, lifecycle state, evidence, and authority. Never match exact wording or a maintainer scenario identifier. Required and conditional capability sets are disjoint. Every declared conditional capability must have a disposition. A genuinely additional proportional owner may be added only from current evidence and requires a complete conditional disposition or an explicit direct-selection justification; it must not duplicate a required, forbidden, or controller owner.
taxonomy_policy: preferred_scale and preferred_risk are evidence-sparse defaults. A normalized alternative is conformant only when it appears in allowed_scales or allowed_risks and repository or task evidence explains the choice. Scale and risk remain independent. Maintainer canary accepted sets are test-only subsets and never runtime instruction. Authority remains exact and never grants consequential effects.

profiles.intent: Select when the requested outcome is a plan for an isolated defect with bounded implementation scope and no edit authority. Do not use for authorized implementation, incident restoration, or a design-only deliverable.

conditional_capabilities.when: the fix changes a contract, compatibility boundary, recovery behavior, or established design
test strategy requires specialist depth beyond the compact plan
the affected behavior reaches sensitive data, privileged boundaries, reliability, operations, infrastructure, or material blast radius

stop_conditions: do not edit files
do not claim an unverified root cause

profiles.intent: Select when repository changes are authorized for a feature or refactor that needs the full build-to-release lifecycle. Do not use for planning-only work, incident containment, migration-specific delivery, or release execution.

conditional_capabilities.when: risk classification identifies security, privacy, reliability, infrastructure, migration, or operational depth
external sources or systems of record require reconciliation or authorized mutation
a meaningful delivery outcome, failure, or reusable learning exists

stop_conditions: implementation waits for Ready and Design gates
publishing requires separate authority

profiles.intent: Select when a broad initiative needs requirements, architecture, dependencies, milestones, and multi-PR or multi-release slicing without implementation authority. Do not use for a bounded one-PR plan or an authorized delivery run.

conditional_capabilities.when: initiative evidence exposes sensitive, privileged, infrastructure, migration, reliability, or high-blast-radius concerns
teams, trackers, external dependencies, status, or communication require synchronization

stop_conditions: do not invent owners or dates
do not implement under planning authority

profiles.intent: Select when the outcome is an independent go, no-go, or conditional release-readiness judgment for a pinned change or PR. Do not use when the user asks to fix findings, prepare missing release material, or execute a release.

conditional_capabilities.when: quality evidence is missing, failing, stale, disputed, quantitative, or requires execution or triage
the diff or release surface is security, privacy, reliability, infrastructure, migration, or operationally material
canonical documentation or release communication is missing or inconsistent

stop_conditions: do not implement fixes
do not self-approve missing evidence

profiles.intent: Select when completed work must be made release-ready through evidence recovery, versioning, notes, rollout, rollback, and gate disposition without consequential release effects. Do not use when merge, tag, publish, deploy, or promotion is authorized for execution.

conditional_capabilities.when: quality evidence is missing, failing, stale, disputed, quantitative, or requires execution or triage
version, changelog, release notes, migration, configuration, runbook, or support knowledge is missing or inconsistent
required independent review or release-readiness adjudication is missing, stale, or unresolved
release status, external systems, handoff, or communication require synchronization

stop_conditions: preparation does not authorize merge, tag, publish, deploy, flag change, or communication

profiles.intent: Select when canonical runbooks, configuration, recovery guidance, and ownership handoff must be changed together for an operational surface. Do not use for ordinary prose-only documentation or a status message with no durable knowledge change.

conditional_capabilities.when: documentation examples, runbook steps, configuration, or recovery procedures require execution or measurable validation

stop_conditions: external document mutation routes through delivery coordination
do not invent support commitments

profiles.intent: Select when the requested deliverable is a solution design or architecture decision grounded in current requirements, with no delivery-plan or implementation authority. Do not use when work breakdown, code changes, or release decisions are part of the requested outcome.

conditional_capabilities.when: the design crosses sensitive, privileged, infrastructure, migration, reliability, or high-blast-radius boundaries

stop_conditions: stop before planning and implementation

profiles.intent: Select when a pinned design, diff, repository state, or artifact needs evidence-backed findings without edits and without an integration or release decision by default. Do not use for implementation, security-led review, or explicit release-readiness adjudication.

conditional_capabilities.when: validation must be executed or quality evidence is missing, failing, stale, disputed, or quantitative
security, privacy, reliability, infrastructure, migration, or operational depth is material
the requested decision includes integration or release readiness

stop_conditions: do not edit
do not suppress uncertain material candidates

profiles.intent: Select when authority is limited to changing canonical user or maintainer documentation and validating its links, examples, build, or rendering. Do not use when code behavior, operational handoff, provider state, or release execution must change.

conditional_capabilities.when: documentation concerns security, privacy, permissions, operations, migration, or recovery
documentation changes a versioned, release, migration, configuration, or operational contract

stop_conditions: do not invent code test results
do not create duplicate documentation

profiles.intent: Select when an active incident has explicit containment or restoration authority and requires a minimal repair plus recovery evidence under compressed gates. Do not use for retrospective incident analysis, an ordinary urgent bug, or a failed-deployment assessment without action authority.

conditional_capabilities.when: repair sequencing, dependencies, ownership, or deferred-gate planning exceed a compact incident checklist
runbooks, status, recovery, configuration, support, or release knowledge changes
time and blast radius permit independent hotfix or release review
provider state, incident status, escalation, handoff, or separately authorized communication requires synchronization
the incident reaches a meaningful recovery, failure, or cancelled-outcome boundary

stop_conditions: production action stays within incident authority
communication requires separate authority

profiles.intent: Select when meeting evidence must be reconciled into confirmed decisions, action candidates, native targets, and authorized drafts or receipts. Do not use when notes themselves are treated as approved requirements or when the primary outcome is implementation.

conditional_capabilities.when: notes contain ambiguous behavior or proposed requirements that need confirmation
confirmed actions require dependency-safe work structure
confirmed decisions or knowledge must update a canonical durable source

stop_conditions: notes do not silently create work
draft authority does not authorize send or write

profiles.intent: Select when repository, tracker, document, or provider records disagree or are stale and the outcome is a provenance-preserving status reconciliation. Do not use for a meeting-specific follow-up, delivery implementation, or an unbounded choice between conflicting sources.

conditional_capabilities.when: repository or project context is missing or contradictory
canonical specification or status documentation must change
reconciliation identifies delivery-plan, dependency, milestone, or ownership changes

stop_conditions: do not silently choose between contradictory sources
no write without exact target and authority

profiles.intent: Select when the review target is explicitly security, privacy, permission, secret, trust-boundary, or abuse-path sensitive and needs independent review closure. Do not use for a general code review with only routine proportional security checks.

conditional_capabilities.when: candidate validation requires execution, measurement, scanner triage, or missing/failing quality evidence
the review must make a release-blocking disposition
findings, suppressions, deferrals, owners, status, handoff, or authorized communication must be synchronized with an external source

stop_conditions: uncertain material candidates remain open or deferred
suppression is not risk acceptance

profiles.intent: Select when nondeterministic, missing, or provider-only CI evidence must be causally triaged at an exact revision before release impact is decided. Do not use for a deterministic local test failure or for changing CI configuration under review-only authority.

conditional_capabilities.when: provider access, external status, escalation, or communication requires cross-system coordination
the failure demonstrates recurring delivery friction or reaches a meaningful failure, recovery, cancellation, or learning boundary

stop_conditions: no blind retry
do not claim CI parity without provider evidence

profiles.intent: Select when a staged deployment or feature-flag rollout has already failed and the requested outcome is incident assessment, containment disposition, and evidence recovery. Do not use for an ordinary release-readiness review or silently assume rollback or redeploy authority.

conditional_capabilities.when: provider state, incident status, escalation, or communication requires synchronization
the incident reaches a meaningful recovery, failure, or cancelled-outcome boundary

stop_conditions: no flag or deployment mutation without authority
pipeline success is not user recovery

profiles.intent: Select when an authorized change includes a data or state migration exercised in a preview-like environment with compatibility, rollback, teardown, and staged-release obligations. Do not use for migration design alone or a production execution request.

conditional_capabilities.when: provider mutation, shared environment ownership, status, handoff, or teardown synchronization is involved
the preview delivery produces a meaningful result, failure, or reusable learning

stop_conditions: do not invent an environment
code rollback does not imply data rollback

profiles.intent: Select when one review claim combines performance, packaging, signing, or distribution evidence and needs a cross-cutting artifact-bound disposition. Do not use when exactly one specialized evidence claim determines the route.

conditional_capabilities.when: the claim requires independent or release-readiness adjudication beyond the evidence owner
validated results change canonical platform, packaging, signing, or distribution guidance
authorized provider reads, distribution status, handoff, escalation, or communication must be synchronized

required_evidence: exact revision, device, runtime, build, data, and run count
stop_conditions: packaging is not signing, notarization, deployment, or distribution
do not generalize from a noisy sample

profiles.intent: Select when the decision turns on a measured performance, capacity, latency, throughput, resource, or regression claim against a pinned baseline. Do not use for broad platform validation or an optimization implementation request.

conditional_capabilities.when: measurement exercises privileged, sensitive, production, resource-exhaustion, or supply-chain surfaces
independent adjudication is required for a release or public claim
the measurement gates a release or artifact decision
validated results change canonical performance guidance or a durable claim

required_evidence: exact revision, device, runtime, build, data, run count, and threshold
stop_conditions: do not modify the product
do not generalize from a noisy or incomparable sample

profiles.intent: Select when a built package's exact identity, provenance, contents, execution behavior, and release suitability must be validated without signing or publication. Do not use for source-only testing or a distribution-channel claim.

conditional_capabilities.when: artifact provenance, permissions, contents, dependencies, or supply-chain exposure is material
independent release-readiness adjudication is required
validated packaging behavior changes canonical build or release guidance

required_evidence: exact revision, build, package path or digest, platform, and packaging command
stop_conditions: package validation does not authorize signing, notarization, publication, deployment, or distribution

profiles.intent: Select when existing signature, certificate, entitlement, trust, or notarization evidence must be audited read-only for a pinned artifact. Do not use when credentials may be accessed or signing, submission, stapling, publication, or distribution is requested.

conditional_capabilities.when: independent attestation or release-readiness adjudication is required
validated results change canonical signing, notarization, or release guidance

expected_artifacts: certificate or identity metadata without secrets
required_evidence: exact revision, artifact digest, platform, signature metadata, and notarization record
stop_conditions: do not access private signing material
do not sign, re-sign, submit, staple, publish, or distribute

profiles.intent: Select when an artifact's current channel presence, installability, integrity, access, and provider state must be validated without changing external state. Do not use for package-only inspection or an authorized publish, deploy, or promotion.

conditional_capabilities.when: authorized provider reads, distribution status, target reconciliation, escalation, or communication is required
independent release-readiness adjudication is required
validated results change canonical distribution or support guidance

required_evidence: exact revision, artifact digest, distribution channel, environment, and current provider state
stop_conditions: do not upload, publish, deploy, promote, distribute, change access, or communicate externally

profiles.intent: Select when a meaningful completed, failed, cancelled, recovered, or repeatedly painful outcome needs evidence-backed learning and bounded follow-up under report-only authority. Do not use for a prospective delivery route or to treat mere code completion as an observed outcome.

conditional_capabilities.when: validated learning changes canonical user, maintainer, operator, architecture, or process guidance
follow-up ownership or system-of-record reconciliation is needed without performing an unauthorized write

stop_conditions: do not invent outcomes or lessons
do not mutate code, release state, trackers, or providers under report-only authority

profiles.intent: Select when exact merge, tag, publish, deploy, promote, or equivalent production-release effects are explicitly authorized for a pinned artifact and environment with resolved gates and rollback. Do not use for preparation, review, provider visibility, or an imprecise request to ship.

conditional_capabilities.when: a required independent approval or release-readiness adjudication is missing, stale, disputed, or unresolved
the executed release reaches a meaningful observed outcome, recovery, failure, or reusable learning boundary

required_evidence: exact commit, artifact digest, environment, provider, and release command or API target
current approvals, quality, security, documentation, migration, observability, and rollback evidence
post-effect readback bound to the released unit
stop_conditions: stop before any effect when target, artifact, authority, gates, or rollback is unresolved
never broaden release authority to another environment, artifact, provider, communication, or follow-up action

profiles.intent: Select when an active workflow, plugin, instruction set, hook, configuration, or branded state is being replaced and then disabled or removed with rollback and fresh-task observation gates. Do not use for archival documentation, a replacement design alone, or uninstalling an unverified target.

conditional_capabilities.when: shared ownership, external systems, status, handoff, or authorized communication must be synchronized
the decommission reaches a meaningful observed success, rollback, failure, or reusable learning boundary

expected_artifacts: exact target identities and recovery sources
static and independently resolved fresh-task smoke evidence
required_evidence: active instructions, prompts, hooks, configurations, state, installed versions, and legacy imperative artifacts
replacement parity at an exact source and installed revision
fresh-task behavior after each disable with reversible recovery evidence
stop_conditions: do not disable or uninstall without exact target authority and a verified recovery source
disable one candidate at a time, re-enable on any routing or parity failure, and uninstall only after the observation gate and user-confirmed scope
